youtube_items.title: [YT] What really happens to the plastic you throw away - Emma Bryce
youtube_items.summary: This is the story of three plastic bottles, empty and discarded. Their journeys are about to diverge, without comes that impact nothing less than the fate of the planet. But they weren't always this way. To understand where these bottles end up, we must first explore their origins. The heroes of our story were conceived in this oil refinery. The plastic in their bodies was formed by chemically bonding oil and gas molecules together to make monomers. In turn, these monomers were bonded into long polymer chains to make plastic in the form of millions of pellets. Those were melted at manufacturing plants and reformed in molds to create the resilient material that makes up the triplets' bodies. Soon after my scuba diving experience, I had to do a science paper back in high school and I decided to study the plastic pollution and how I could potentially clean this up. And the original idea was to have a very long floating barrier in the middle of the ocean attached to the sea beds that would use the natural current of the ocean to collect the plastic. analysis.problem: Insufficient waste management and plastic pollution
analysis.root_cause: Lack of effective waste management infrastructure and inadequate regulation of plastic production and disposal
leverage_points: Government policies and regulations on waste management and plastic production
Consumer behavior and awareness of plastic pollution

proposed_actions.action: Implement extended producer responsibility for plastic production and disposal
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Reduce plastic waste in oceans by 50% within 5 years

proposed_actions.action: Develop and implement effective waste management infrastructure in low- to middle-income countries
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Increase waste collection rates by 30% within 3 years

evidence: About 0.5% of all plastic waste ends up in the ocean
Low- to middle-income countries tend to have inadequate waste management infrastructure
analysis.generalization: This problem connects to the domains of Planet, Human, and Civilization, as it affects not only the environment but also human health and the economy
analysis.summary: Insufficient waste management and plastic pollution are significant environmental problems that require immediate attention and action. Effective waste management infrastructure and regulation of plastic production and disposal are necessary to mitigate this issue.

raw_items.title: SWANA Releases New ARF Report on Horizontal Landfill Gas Collection Systems
raw_items.summary: The SWANA Applied Research Foundation (ARF) released a new report titled Horizontal Landfill Gas Collection Systems. This report provides updated guidance to assist solid waste professionals in designing, installing, and operating horizontal landfill gas (LFG) collection systems.
